description: Fix three validated bugs in the Chrome extension: a race condition in browser_execute_script async result polling, an optimistic UI rollback race in PluginCard tool toggles, and missing error handling in the waitForElement poll interval.

userStories.title: Namespace browser_execute_script async results by execution ID
userStories.description: As a developer using browser_execute_script, I want concurrent script executions to not collide so that each execution reliably returns its own result.
acceptanceCriteria: Each execution stores its result under a unique key derived from the exec file UUID (e.g., `__openTabs.__execResult_<uuid>`) instead of the shared `__openTabs.__lastExecResult`
The polling loop reads only the namespaced key for its own execution
Cleanup (on success, timeout, and cancellation) deletes only the namespaced key, not other executions' results
The `__lastExecAsync` flag is similarly namespaced per execution
The exec wrapper template (in platform/plugin-tools or platform/mcp-server, wherever it generates the __exec-<uuid>.js file) writes to the namespaced key matching the UUID in the filename
userStories.notes:
The bug is in `platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/extension-commands.ts`, function `handleBrowserExecuteScript` (lines 352-482).

The exec file is named `__exec-<uuid>.js` — the UUID is already available in the `execFile` param (line 359). Extract the UUID from the filename with a regex like `/^__exec-([a-f0-9-]+)\.js$/`.

The injected polling script (line 388-428) currently reads/writes `__openTabs.__lastExecResult` and `__openTabs.__lastExecAsync`. Change these to `__openTabs[`__execResult_${uuid}`]` and `__openTabs[`__execAsync_${uuid}`]`. Pass the UUID as an additional arg to the injected functions.

The exec wrapper file that sets these globals is generated by the MCP server. Search for `__lastExecResult` across the codebase to find where the wrapper template is defined (likely in `platform/mcp-server/src/`). Update it to write to the namespaced key. The wrapper filename already contains the UUID — extract it from the script's own filename or pass it as a variable in the wrapper template.

The timeout cleanup (lines 446-458) and the cancellation guard (line 384, 445) must also use the namespaced keys.

This is marked opus because the fix spans two packages (browser-extension and mcp-server) and requires understanding the exec file generation pipeline.

userStories.title: Fix PluginCard optimistic rollback overwriting concurrent toggles
userStories.description: As a user toggling tools rapidly in the side panel, I want my most recent toggle action to be preserved so that earlier failed requests do not silently revert my latest choice.
acceptanceCriteria: A monotonically increasing counter ref is added to PluginCard
Each toggle (handleToggleAll, handleToggleTool) increments the counter and captures its value before the optimistic update
On server error, the rollback only applies if the counter has not been incremented since (i.e., no newer toggle has occurred)
If a newer toggle occurred, the stale rollback is skipped entirely

userStories.title: Add try-catch to waitForElement poll interval callback
userStories.description: As a developer using browser_wait_for_element, I want DOM errors during polling to produce a clear error message instead of a confusing 25-second timeout.
acceptanceCriteria: The setInterval callback inside the injected script for handleBrowserWaitForElement is wrapped in a try-catch
On catch: clearInterval is called, and the promise resolves with an error message including the caught exception's message
